risk_if_not_implemented: Without Information Sharing With Third Parties, third-party risks may go unmanaged, creating supply-chain exposure that compromises the organization.

compensating_control_1.name: Third-Party Contract Requirements
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to require contractual requirements for applicable security, compliance and resilience requirements with third-parties, reflecting the organization's needs to protect its Technology Assets, Applications, Services and/or Data (TAASD).
compensating_control_1.justification: Third-Party Contract Requirements (TPM-05) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of Information Sharing With Third Parties (PRI-07) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the data-handling focus of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Information Sharing
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize a process to assist users in making information sharing decisions to ensure data is appropriately protected.
compensating_control_2.justification: Information Sharing (DCH-14) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Information Sharing With Third Parties (PRI-07) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Data applicability. Given the data-handling focus of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
